rule_text: Government servant who buys AP stores WITHOUT calling for tenders, when he was bound by Article 125 to call for tenders, is LIABLE to be called upon by superior authority or Accountant-General to JUSTIFY the method of purchase adopted.
verification_method: If tender method waived: justification on file matching Article 125 exception clause; otherwise audit-callable

rule_text: On opening tenders for AP stores, the Government servant must record on the spot a brief statement giving names of tenderers and amounts of their tenders, AND obtain dated signature of those tenderers present in token of presence; if the lowest tender is not accepted, recorded reasons must be available to AG during local inspection.
verification_method: Check tender opening minutes: tenderer names + amounts + dated signatures present-bidders; L1-bypass reasons recorded

rule_text: NO TENDER shall be accepted in AP from any person DIRECTLY OR INDIRECTLY connected with the Government service; if collusion is detected between a tenderer and Government servant, it should be DEALT WITH SEVERELY.
source_clause: AP Financial Code Vol-I, Article 127 — GO Ms No 62 / Finance (Accts) dated 25-2-1969
verification_method: Check bidder declaration of no Government-service connection; verify against employee/relatives database for COI

rule_text: REASONS FOR REJECTING any AP tender shall NOT be communicated to any tenderer — no tenderer has any right to be told the reasons for rejecting his tender.
verification_method: Check rejection-letter format does NOT contain bid-evaluation-reasoning; reasons recorded internally for AG only

rule_text: When a tender is accepted for AP stores supply, the successful tenderer must execute an AGREEMENT; LONG-TERM CONTRACTS should be AVOIDED; only specifically authorised Government servants may sign agreements on behalf of the Governor; FIXED PRICE per article is essential — agreements MUST NOT provide for prices fluctuating with the market price.
verification_method: Check supply agreement: fixed price + signed by authorised officer + no market-fluctuation clause + duration not unreasonably long

rule_text: No order should be placed for any AP stores without obtaining at least a written agreement from the supplier as to the price, if not a formal written contract; supply agreements are liable to STAMP DUTY but their REGISTRATION is OPTIONAL.
verification_method: Check tender supply order has written price agreement; verify stamp-duty paid; registration not mandatory

rule_text: AP private firms/persons making contracts with Government must furnish SECURITY for due fulfillment equivalent to 10% OF THE TOTAL VALUE of the contract; Head of Department may exempt firms of established repute; security may take any of the forms (1)-(5) in Article 279; personal security of two persons of known probity may be accepted in exceptional cases.
verification_method: Check Performance Security/PBG = 10% of contract value; reject lower without HOD repute-exemption order
